title: TDD: fix gascity #793
description:
Track GH issue gastownhall/gascity#793: scale_check / work_query commands are not template-expanded — `{{.Rig}}` passed verbatim, breaks rig-scoped pool agents.

## The bug
scale_check and work_query fields in agent config accept template vars like `{{.Rig}}` but the substitution is not actually run — the literal string `{{.Rig}}` is passed to the command and breaks rig-scoped pool queries.

## Constraints
- **TDD**: test that an agent with `scale_check = "bd list --rig {{.Rig}} ..."` expands to the rig name at invocation time.
- Fix on PR branch; never push to main directly.

## Starting points
- scale_check / work_query invocation path — grep internal/
- Existing template expansion infra for agent args (the one that DOES work for dir, workDir, etc.)

## Deliverable
PR with regression test + fix + references #793. New bugs → new issues.

## HARD RULE — READ CAREFULLY
**You MUST NOT merge to main.** Open a PR and STOP. Do not run `gh pr merge`, do not squash-merge, do not push to main, do not add yourself as a reviewer. A human will review and merge. If a push to main is the only apparent option, abort and leave the PR for human review.
notes: Already fixed by PR #860 (commits 173a0785, 03ae26a6) which added expandAgentCommandTemplate and wired it into the scale_check and work_query hot paths in build_desired_state.go and work_query_probe.go. Verified: tests in cmd/gc/probe_template_test.go cover {{.Rig}}, {{.AgentBase}}, literal-byte-identical, parse error, unknown field, and nil agent cases — all pass on feat/completion-dynamic-args. GH issue #793 is CLOSED.

title: TDD: fix gascity #720
description:
Track GH issue gastownhall/gascity#720: witness salvage + resling leaves zombie molecules; sling has no source-bead mutex, allowing parallel polecats on the same bead.

## The bug
Two coupled problems:
1. Witness "salvage" operation can re-sling a bead while the original molecule is still running → zombie molecule + parallel polecats.
2. `gc sling` has no per-source-bead mutex, so two concurrent slings can race on the same bead.

## Constraints
- **TDD**: two tests — (a) witness salvage while molecule active must no-op or cleanly cancel old molecule; (b) concurrent sling of same bead must serialize (one wins, one skips/waits).
- Fix on PR branch; never push to main directly.

## Starting points
- Witness salvage logic — grep internal/ for "salvage"
- `gc sling` command handler in cmd_sling.go
- Bead-level advisory lock pattern

## Deliverable
PR with 2 regression tests + both fixes + references #720. New bugs → new issues.

## HARD RULE — READ CAREFULLY
**You MUST NOT merge to main.** Open a PR and STOP. Do not run `gh pr merge`, do not squash-merge, do not push to main, do not add yourself as a reviewer. A human will review and merge. If a push to main is the only apparent option, abort and leave the PR for human review.
notes: Already resolved by PR #852 (merged 2026-04-18), which added internal/sourceworkflow with live-workflow singleton (ListLiveRoots) and per-source-bead cross-process lock (WithLock). GH #720 closed 2026-04-19. Both required regression tests exist and pass on main: (a) TestSlingAttachGraphFormulaRejectsExistingLiveRoot verifies a second sling on a source bead with a live workflow returns ConflictError (covers witness salvage no-op / no zombie molecules). (b) TestSlingAttachGraphFormulaConcurrentLaunchCreatesSingleRoot verifies two concurrent AttachFormula calls on the same source produce exactly one live root. Verified: go test ./internal/sourceworkflow/... and the targeted sling tests all PASS. No code changes needed; no PR opened.

title: TDD: fix gascity #774
description:
Track GH issue gastownhall/gascity#774: {{.AgentBase}} resolves to template name, not namepool slot — pool instances collide on one worktree path.

## The bug
Template substitution of `{{.AgentBase}}` returns the template's base name, not the per-instance namepool slot (e.g. "opus-1", "opus-2"). Multiple pool instances therefore share a single worktree path, stomping each other's work.

## Constraints
- **TDD**: table-driven test that instantiates N pool workers off one template, asserts each gets a UNIQUE worktree path derived from namepool slot.
- Fix on PR branch; never push to main directly.
- Understand the difference between AgentBase, AgentName, and namepool slot/instance identifiers. Fix the template var to use the right one.

## Starting points
- Grep `\.AgentBase` in internal/
- Template expansion code path
- Namepool slot allocation — probably in internal/pool/ or similar

## Deliverable
PR with regression test + fix + references #774. New bugs → new issues.

## HARD RULE — READ CAREFULLY
**You MUST NOT merge to main.** Open a PR and STOP. Do not run `gh pr merge`, do not squash-merge, do not push to main, do not add yourself as a reviewer. A human will review and merge. If a push to main is the only apparent option, abort and leave the PR for human review.

title: TDD: fix gascity #744
description:
Track GH issue gastownhall/gascity#744: gc handoff self-handoff crashes human-attended (named) sessions on PreCompact.

## The bug
When a human-attended named session (e.g. mayor) hits PreCompact, the configured hook runs `gc handoff "context cycle"` to restart itself. This crashes the human's claude session under some conditions.

## Constraints
- **TDD**: reproduce the crash in a test first — simulate PreCompact event on a named session with handoff hook, assert session survives cleanly.
- Fix on PR branch; never push to main directly.
- Preserve handoff semantics for autonomous sessions. Only fix the named-session-attended path.

## Starting points
- Grep `gc handoff` callers and `PreCompact` matcher
- `cmd/gc/cmd_handoff.go` likely
- Settings: hooks/claude.json has `PreCompact` entry running `gc handoff "context cycle"`

## Deliverable
PR with regression test + fix + references #744. New bugs → new issues.

## HARD RULE — READ CAREFULLY
**You MUST NOT merge to main.** Open a PR and STOP. Do not run `gh pr merge`, do not squash-merge, do not push to main, do not add yourself as a reviewer. A human will review and merge. If a push to main is the only apparent option, abort and leave the PR for human review.
close_reason: No-op: #744 already fixed on main via merged PR #927 + follow-up 84c0e19d. Regression test TestDoHandoff_Regression744_NamedSessionSkipsRestart passes. Bead created 2026-04-18, PR merged 2026-04-19 - stale assignment.

title: TDD: fix gascity #663
description:
Track GH issue gastownhall/gascity#663: supervisor restarts entire city on transient Dolt read failure (packets.go:58 unexpected EOF).

## The bug
When reconciler encounters transient Dolt error during session-bead metadata write, current code issues `op=interrupt wave=0` to EVERY managed session (dogs, polecats, deacon, witness, mayor), then restarts the city. 73 occurrences observed in 37h. Each restart takes ~65s and loses in-flight work.

## Constraints
- **TDD**: write a failing test FIRST — inject `unexpected EOF` on ONE SetMetadataBatch call with a fake/wrapped store, assert supervisor retries-or-skips rather than interrupting unrelated sessions.
- Fix on a PR branch against gastownhall/gascity main. **Never push to main — always PR.**
- Target blast radius: isolate per-bead failures. Full city restart OK only if Dolt is truly down (port unreachable for N sec after retry).
- Do NOT fix the EOF root cause (tracked in #525/#560). Make supervisor resilient.

## Starting points
- `cmd/gc/session_beads.go:1136` — setMetaBatch emits "session beads: setting metadata on %s: %v"
- `cmd/gc/session_lifecycle_parallel.go:97` — "session lifecycle: op=%s wave=%d" emitter. Trace callers back to find the decision to interrupt-all.
- `internal/api/client.go` already has `isTransientDoltError` — reusable.

## Deliverable
PR on gastownhall/gascity with: regression test + per-bead retry/skip logic + `make check` passes + references #663. Any NEW bugs uncovered → open separate issues.

## HARD RULE — READ CAREFULLY
**You MUST NOT merge to main.** Open a PR and STOP. Do not run `gh pr merge`, do not squash-merge, do not push to main, do not add yourself as a reviewer. A human will review and merge. If a push to main is the only apparent option, abort and leave the PR for human review.
notes: Opened https://github.com/gastownhall/gascity/pull/1123 with regression test codifying per-bead SetMetadataBatch isolation on transient unexpected-EOF. Adds eofOnBeadStore wrapper + TestSyncSessionBeads_IsolatesSetMetadataBatchEOFToSingleBead; asserts stderr logs the failed bead ID, unrelated beads still have template backfilled, and the failed bead stays unwritten for next-tick retry. Production cascade path was already fixed by PR #937 (safeTick panic recovery); this test guards the companion skip-via-continue contract in syncSessionBeads so future refactors cannot regress it. No production code change. make check pre-existing failures (TestCityRuntimeReload*) reproduce on clean main and are unrelated.

title: Push feature branch and open PR
description:
Push your feature branch to origin and open a PR against base_branch.
You cease to exist after this step.

**1. Final clean-state verification (safeguard):**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
If ANY output (untracked files, uncommitted changes):
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "chore: capture remaining work (ga-62x)"
```

**2. Rebase on latest base_branch (one last time) to minimize PR merge conflicts:**
```bash
git fetch origin main
git rebase origin/main
# If rebase conflicts: resolve them, then git rebase --continue. If
# conflicts are unresolvable, mail witness and drain-ack.
```

**3. Push the feature branch:**
```bash
BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
```

If push fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Push failed" -m "Issue: ga-62x. Branch: $BRANCH. Cannot push to origin."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**4. Open the PR via gh:**

Derive a title and body from the bead. Keep the title short (≤70 chars);
put detail in the body.
```bash
BEAD_TITLE=$(bd show ga-62x --json | jq -r '.title')
BEAD_ID="ga-62x"

# Title: "<bead title> (<bead id>)" — truncate if too long
PR_TITLE="${BEAD_TITLE} (${BEAD_ID})"

# Body: ID + full description + footer
PR_BODY=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.description // ""')

gh pr create --base "main" --head "$BRANCH" --title "$PR_TITLE" --body "$(cat <<EOF
$PR_BODY

---

Closes $BEAD_ID (polecat-pr).
EOF
)"
```

Capture the PR URL for the close-note:
```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

If `gh pr create` fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: gh pr create failed" -m "Issue: ga-62x. Branch: $BRANCH. PR did not open."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**5. Close the bead with a pointer to the PR:**
```bash
bd update ga-62x --notes "PR opened: $PR_URL (branch: $BRANCH)"
bd close ga-62x
```

**6. Tear down the worktree.**

The worktree lives on until here so restarts during push/PR can resume.
Now it's safe to remove:
```bash
WORKTREE_PATH=$(pwd)
cd ..
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE_PATH" --force || rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-62x --unset-metadata work_dir
# Leave metadata.branch intact — it's useful post-merge for traceability.
```

**7. Signal reconciler and exit.**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
exit
```

`gc runtime drain-ack` tells the reconciler to kill this session. The
reconciler only restarts you if the pool check command finds more work.
You are GONE. Done means gone. There is no idle state.

**Exit criteria:** Feature branch pushed, PR opened against
main, worktree removed, bead closed, session exited.

title: Self-review and run tests
description:
Review your changes and verify they work.

**Config: setup_command = **
**Config: typecheck_command = **
**Config: lint_command = **
**Config: build_command = **
**Config: test_command = **

**1. Review the diff:**
```bash
git diff origin/main...HEAD
git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD
git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD
```

Check for: bugs, security issues, style violations, missing error handling,
debug cruft, unintended file changes. Fix anything you find.

**2. Run quality checks (skip empty commands):**
```bash





```

**ALL CHECKS MUST PASS.** If your change caused the failure, fix it.
If pre-existing, file a bead.

**3. Ensure everything is committed:**
```bash
git status                  # Must be clean
git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline  # Must show your commits
```

If uncommitted changes exist:
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "<type>: <description> (ga-62x)"
```

NEVER discard implementation changes with `git checkout -- .`

**Exit criteria:** All checks pass, all changes committed, working tree clean.

title: Implement the solution
description:
Do the actual implementation work.

**Working principles:**
- Follow existing codebase conventions
- Make atomic, focused commits
- Keep changes scoped to the assigned issue
- Don't gold-plate or scope-creep

**If resuming a rejected branch:** Read `metadata.rejection_reason`
from load-context. Focus on fixing the specific issue that caused
rejection — don't redo everything.

**Commit frequently:**
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description> (ga-62x)"
```

Commit types: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore

**Discovered work (outside scope):**
```bash
bd create --title "Found: <description>" --type bug --priority 2
```
Do NOT fix unrelated issues in this branch.

**If stuck (>15 minutes):**
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Stuck on implementation" -m "Issue: ga-62x
Problem: <what's blocking you>
Tried: <what you've attempted>"
```

**If context filling up:**
```bash
gc runtime request-restart
```
This blocks until the controller kills your session. The next session
resumes from context (re-reads formula steps, checks git/bead state).

**Exit criteria:** Implementation complete, all changes committed.

title: Verify pre-flights pass on base branch
description:
Check if the codebase is healthy BEFORE starting your work.

**Config: typecheck_command = **
**Config: lint_command = **
**Config: test_command = **

**Skip this step if resuming a rejected branch** — pre-flights were
already verified on the prior attempt. Close this step and proceed.

**1. Run pre-flights (skip empty commands silently):**
```bash



```

**2. If pre-flights pass:** proceed.

**3. If pre-flights fail on main:**

File a bead and proceed. Do NOT fix pre-existing failures — that's
not your assignment.

FORBIDDEN: Pushing to main. FORBIDDEN: Fixing pre-existing failures.

```bash
bd create --title "Pre-existing failure: <description>" --type bug --priority 1
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "NOTICE: main has failing pre-flights" -m "Filed: <bead-id>. Proceeding with ga-62x."
```

**Exit criteria:** Pre-flights pass (or pre-existing bug filed), ready to implement.

title: Set up worktree on a feature branch
description:
Create an isolated worktree checked out on a new feature branch off
base_branch. The feature branch will be pushed and turned into a PR
in the terminal step.

**Config: base_branch = main**
**Config: setup_command = **

**1. Fetch latest:**
```bash
git fetch --prune origin
```

**2. Resolve worktree path and branch name from metadata (resume case).**

Check if `metadata.work_dir` already records your worktree path, and if
`metadata.branch` records your feature branch name:
```bash
WORKTREE=$(bd show ga-62x --json | jq -r '.metadata.work_dir // empty')
BRANCH=$(bd show ga-62x --json | jq -r '.metadata.branch // empty')
```

**If worktree path exists in metadata and directory still exists** — reuse it:
```bash
cd "$WORKTREE"
git fetch origin main
# If branch has unpushed local work, rebase it on base_branch; else stay on branch
git rebase origin/main
```
If the directory is missing, fall through to create a new one.

**If no worktree** — create one:
```bash
# Derive a branch name if not already recorded
if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
    SLUG=$(echo "ga-62x" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -c 'a-z0-9-' '-' | sed 's/-\+/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//')
    BRANCH="users/${GC_AGENT:-polecat}/${SLUG}"
fi

WORKTREE_PATH=$(mktemp -d /tmp/polecat-ga-62x-XXXXXX)
rmdir "$WORKTREE_PATH"
# Create feature branch off latest base_branch, check out in the new worktree
git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" "origin/main"
cd "$WORKTREE_PATH"
```

Record immediately so restarts and witness recovery can find both:
```bash
bd update ga-62x --set-metadata work_dir="$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-62x --set-metadata branch="$BRANCH"
```

**3. Ensure clean working state:**
```bash
git status                  # Should be clean
git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD   # Should show your feature branch
```

**4. Run project setup (if configured):**
```bash

```
Empty setup_command → skip.

**Exit criteria:** In your `/tmp/polecat-*` worktree, on your feature
branch, deps installed, work_dir + branch recorded on the bead.

title: Load context and verify assignment
description:
Initialize your session and understand your assignment.

**1. Prime your environment:**
```bash
gc prime                    # Load role context
bd prime                    # Load beads context
```

**2. Check your hook:**
```bash
bd list --assignee=$GC_AGENT --status=in_progress
```

The hook_bead is your assigned issue. Read it carefully:
```bash
bd show ga-62x           # Full issue details
bd show ga-62x --json | jq '.metadata'  # Check for existing metadata
```

**3. Check for rejection (IMPORTANT):**

If `metadata.rejection_reason` exists, this bead was previously attempted
and rejected by the refinery. Read the reason carefully:
- Rebase conflict → you'll resume the existing branch and rebase
- Test failure → you'll resume the branch and fix the issue

If `metadata.branch` exists, a branch already exists from the prior attempt.
You will use it in workspace-setup instead of creating a new one.

**4. Check inbox for additional context:**
```bash
gc mail inbox
# Read any HANDOFF or assignment messages, then archive after absorbing context
# gc mail read <id> → process → gc mail archive <id>
```

**5. Understand the requirements:**
- What exactly needs to be done?
- What files are likely involved?
- Are there dependencies or blockers?
- What does "done" look like?
- If rejected: what specifically needs fixing?

If blocked or unclear, mail Witness:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Unclear requirements" -m "Issue: ga-62x
Question: <what you need clarified>"
```

**Exit criteria:** You understand the work and can begin.

title: mol-polecat-pr
description:
Polecat PR-review variant — commits to a feature branch and opens a pull
request against base_branch.

Extends mol-polecat-base with a feature-branch workspace setup (isolated
/tmp worktree + branch off base_branch) and a commit-push-PR terminal
step instead of direct-to-base commit. Designed for cities that use
human or automated PR review (e.g., MCDClient) before merge.

## Polecat Contract (PR-Review Model)

1. Receive work (molecule poured with this formula, assigned to you)
2. Follow steps in order (read descriptions, execute, move to next)
3. Commit to feature branch, push, open PR, close bead, exit
4. You are GONE — review + merge happens downstream

**Feature branch + PR.** Work lands on `users/<agent>/<issue-slug>`
branch in a /tmp worktree that is isolated from the shared rig tree.
The PR is opened against base_branch via `gh pr create`.

## Why /tmp worktrees

Pool agents sharing a single rig working tree stomp each other: one
agent's `git checkout <branch>` wipes another agent's untracked files
or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |
close_reason: orphan: sling created polecat molecule without setting gc.routed_to metadata; never claimed by a worker

title: review: cmd/gc subcommand flag surface — inconsistencies between subcommands for common flags (--city, --rig, --json)? Report only.
notes: Released from dead worker session  — pool workers went zombie overnight, no tmux session alive, releasing for re-claim

title: Push feature branch and open PR
description:
Push your feature branch to origin and open a PR against base_branch.
You cease to exist after this step.

**1. Final clean-state verification (safeguard):**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
If ANY output (untracked files, uncommitted changes):
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "chore: capture remaining work (ga-x1q)"
```

**2. Rebase on latest base_branch (one last time) to minimize PR merge conflicts:**
```bash
git fetch origin main
git rebase origin/main
# If rebase conflicts: resolve them, then git rebase --continue. If
# conflicts are unresolvable, mail witness and drain-ack.
```

**3. Push the feature branch:**
```bash
BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
```

If push fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Push failed" -m "Issue: ga-x1q. Branch: $BRANCH. Cannot push to origin."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**4. Open the PR via gh:**

Derive a title and body from the bead. Keep the title short (≤70 chars);
put detail in the body.
```bash
BEAD_TITLE=$(bd show ga-x1q --json | jq -r '.title')
BEAD_ID="ga-x1q"

# Title: "<bead title> (<bead id>)" — truncate if too long
PR_TITLE="${BEAD_TITLE} (${BEAD_ID})"

# Body: ID + full description + footer
PR_BODY=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.description // ""')

gh pr create --base "main" --head "$BRANCH" --title "$PR_TITLE" --body "$(cat <<EOF
$PR_BODY

---

Closes $BEAD_ID (polecat-pr).
EOF
)"
```

Capture the PR URL for the close-note:
```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

If `gh pr create` fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: gh pr create failed" -m "Issue: ga-x1q. Branch: $BRANCH. PR did not open."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**5. Close the bead with a pointer to the PR:**
```bash
bd update ga-x1q --notes "PR opened: $PR_URL (branch: $BRANCH)"
bd close ga-x1q
```

**6. Tear down the worktree.**

The worktree lives on until here so restarts during push/PR can resume.
Now it's safe to remove:
```bash
WORKTREE_PATH=$(pwd)
cd ..
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE_PATH" --force || rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-x1q --unset-metadata work_dir
# Leave metadata.branch intact — it's useful post-merge for traceability.
```

**7. Signal reconciler and exit.**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
exit
```

`gc runtime drain-ack` tells the reconciler to kill this session. The
reconciler only restarts you if the pool check command finds more work.
You are GONE. Done means gone. There is no idle state.

**Exit criteria:** Feature branch pushed, PR opened against
main, worktree removed, bead closed, session exited.

```bash
git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD
```

Check for: bugs, security issues, style violations, missing error handling,
debug cruft, unintended file changes. Fix anything you find.

**2. Run quality checks (skip empty commands):**
```bash





```

**ALL CHECKS MUST PASS.** If your change caused the failure, fix it.
If pre-existing, file a bead.

**3. Ensure everything is committed:**
```bash
git status                  # Must be clean
git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline  # Must show your commits
```

If uncommitted changes exist:
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "<type>: <description> (ga-x1q)"
```

NEVER discard implementation changes with `git checkout -- .`

**Exit criteria:** All checks pass, all changes committed, working tree clean.

title: Implement the solution
description:
Do the actual implementation work.

**Working principles:**
- Follow existing codebase conventions
- Make atomic, focused commits
- Keep changes scoped to the assigned issue
- Don't gold-plate or scope-creep

**If resuming a rejected branch:** Read `metadata.rejection_reason`
from load-context. Focus on fixing the specific issue that caused
rejection — don't redo everything.

**Commit frequently:**
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description> (ga-x1q)"
```

Commit types: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore

**Discovered work (outside scope):**
```bash
bd create --title "Found: <description>" --type bug --priority 2
```
Do NOT fix unrelated issues in this branch.

**If stuck (>15 minutes):**
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Stuck on implementation" -m "Issue: ga-x1q
Problem: <what's blocking you>
Tried: <what you've attempted>"
```

**If context filling up:**
```bash
gc runtime request-restart
```
This blocks until the controller kills your session. The next session
resumes from context (re-reads formula steps, checks git/bead state).

**Exit criteria:** Implementation complete, all changes committed.


**Exit criteria:** Pre-flights pass (or pre-existing bug filed), ready to implement.

title: Set up worktree on a feature branch
description:
Create an isolated worktree checked out on a new feature branch off
base_branch. The feature branch will be pushed and turned into a PR
in the terminal step.

**Config: base_branch = main**
**Config: setup_command = **

**1. Fetch latest:**
```bash
git fetch --prune origin
```

**2. Resolve worktree path and branch name from metadata (resume case).**

Check if `metadata.work_dir` already records your worktree path, and if
`metadata.branch` records your feature branch name:
```bash
WORKTREE=$(bd show ga-x1q --json | jq -r '.metadata.work_dir // empty')
BRANCH=$(bd show ga-x1q --json | jq -r '.metadata.branch // empty')
```

**If worktree path exists in metadata and directory still exists** — reuse it:
```bash
cd "$WORKTREE"
git fetch origin main
# If branch has unpushed local work, rebase it on base_branch; else stay on branch
git rebase origin/main
```
If the directory is missing, fall through to create a new one.

**If no worktree** — create one:
```bash
# Derive a branch name if not already recorded
if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
    SLUG=$(echo "ga-x1q" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -c 'a-z0-9-' '-' | sed 's/-\+/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//')
    BRANCH="users/${GC_AGENT:-polecat}/${SLUG}"
fi

WORKTREE_PATH=$(mktemp -d /tmp/polecat-ga-x1q-XXXXXX)
rmdir "$WORKTREE_PATH"
# Create feature branch off latest base_branch, check out in the new worktree
git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" "origin/main"
cd "$WORKTREE_PATH"
```

Record immediately so restarts and witness recovery can find both:
```bash
bd update ga-x1q --set-metadata work_dir="$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-x1q --set-metadata branch="$BRANCH"
```

**3. Ensure clean working state:**
```bash
git status                  # Should be clean
git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD   # Should show your feature branch
```

**4. Run project setup (if configured):**
```bash

```
Empty setup_command → skip.

**Exit criteria:** In your `/tmp/polecat-*` worktree, on your feature
branch, deps installed, work_dir + branch recorded on the bead.

title: Load context and verify assignment
description:
Initialize your session and understand your assignment.

**1. Prime your environment:**
```bash
gc prime                    # Load role context
bd prime                    # Load beads context
```

**2. Check your hook:**
```bash
bd list --assignee=$GC_AGENT --status=in_progress
```

The hook_bead is your assigned issue. Read it carefully:
```bash
bd show ga-x1q           # Full issue details
bd show ga-x1q --json | jq '.metadata'  # Check for existing metadata
```

**3. Check for rejection (IMPORTANT):**

If `metadata.rejection_reason` exists, this bead was previously attempted
and rejected by the refinery. Read the reason carefully:
- Rebase conflict → you'll resume the existing branch and rebase
- Test failure → you'll resume the branch and fix the issue

If `metadata.branch` exists, a branch already exists from the prior attempt.
You will use it in workspace-setup instead of creating a new one.

**4. Check inbox for additional context:**
```bash
gc mail inbox
# Read any HANDOFF or assignment messages, then archive after absorbing context
# gc mail read <id> → process → gc mail archive <id>
```

**5. Understand the requirements:**
- What exactly needs to be done?
- What files are likely involved?
- Are there dependencies or blockers?
- What does "done" look like?
- If rejected: what specifically needs fixing?

If blocked or unclear, mail Witness:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Unclear requirements" -m "Issue: ga-x1q
Question: <what you need clarified>"
```

**Exit criteria:** You understand the work and can begin.

title: mol-polecat-pr
description:
Polecat PR-review variant — commits to a feature branch and opens a pull
request against base_branch.

Extends mol-polecat-base with a feature-branch workspace setup (isolated
/tmp worktree + branch off base_branch) and a commit-push-PR terminal
step instead of direct-to-base commit. Designed for cities that use
human or automated PR review (e.g., MCDClient) before merge.

## Polecat Contract (PR-Review Model)

1. Receive work (molecule poured with this formula, assigned to you)
2. Follow steps in order (read descriptions, execute, move to next)
3. Commit to feature branch, push, open PR, close bead, exit
4. You are GONE — review + merge happens downstream

**Feature branch + PR.** Work lands on `users/<agent>/<issue-slug>`
branch in a /tmp worktree that is isolated from the shared rig tree.
The PR is opened against base_branch via `gh pr create`.

## Why /tmp worktrees

Pool agents sharing a single rig working tree stomp each other: one
agent's `git checkout <branch>` wipes another agent's untracked files
or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

title: Set up worktree on a feature branch
description:
Create an isolated worktree checked out on a new feature branch off
base_branch. The feature branch will be pushed and turned into a PR
in the terminal step.

**1. Fetch latest:**
```bash

```

**2. Resolve worktree path and branch name from metadata (resume case).**

Check if `metadata.work_dir` already records your worktree path, and if
`metadata.branch` records your feature branch name:
```bash
WORKTREE=$(bd show ga-m0m --json | jq -r '.metadata.work_dir // empty')
BRANCH=$(bd show ga-m0m --json | jq -r '.metadata.branch // empty')
```

**If worktree path exists in metadata and directory still exists** — reuse it:
```bash
cd "$WORKTREE"
git fetch origin main
# If branch has unpushed local work, rebase it on base_branch; else stay on branch
git rebase origin/main
```
If the directory is missing, fall through to create a new one.

**If no worktree** — create one:
```bash
# Derive a branch name if not already recorded
if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
    SLUG=$(echo "ga-m0m" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -c 'a-z0-9-' '-' | sed 's/-\+/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//')

cd "$WORKTREE_PATH"
```

title: mol-polecat-pr
description:
Polecat PR-review variant — commits to a feature branch and opens a pull
request against base_branch.

Extends mol-polecat-base with a feature-branch workspace setup (isolated
/tmp worktree + branch off base_branch) and a commit-push-PR terminal
step instead of direct-to-base commit. Designed for cities that use
human or automated PR review (e.g., MCDClient) before merge.

## Polecat Contract (PR-Review Model)

1. Receive work (molecule poured with this formula, assigned to you)
2. Follow steps in order (read descriptions, execute, move to next)
3. Commit to feature branch, push, open PR, close bead, exit
4. You are GONE — review + merge happens downstream

**Feature branch + PR.** Work lands on `users/<agent>/<issue-slug>`
branch in a /tmp worktree that is isolated from the shared rig tree.
The PR is opened against base_branch via `gh pr create`.

## Why /tmp worktrees

Pool agents sharing a single rig working tree stomp each other: one
agent's `git checkout <branch>` wipes another agent's untracked files
or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

title: Push feature branch and open PR
description:
Push your feature branch to origin and open a PR against base_branch.
You cease to exist after this step.

**1. Final clean-state verification (safeguard):**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
If ANY output (untracked files, uncommitted changes):
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "chore: capture remaining work (ga-w1u)"
```

**2. Rebase on latest base_branch (one last time) to minimize PR merge conflicts:**
```bash
git fetch origin main
git rebase origin/main
# If rebase conflicts: resolve them, then git rebase --continue. If
# conflicts are unresolvable, mail witness and drain-ack.
```

**3. Push the feature branch:**
```bash
BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
```

If push fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Push failed" -m "Issue: ga-w1u. Branch: $BRANCH. Cannot push to origin."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**4. Open the PR via gh:**

Derive a title and body from the bead. Keep the title short (≤70 chars);
put detail in the body.
```bash
BEAD_TITLE=$(bd show ga-w1u --json | jq -r '.title')
BEAD_ID="ga-w1u"

# Title: "<bead title> (<bead id>)" — truncate if too long
PR_TITLE="${BEAD_TITLE} (${BEAD_ID})"

# Body: ID + full description + footer
PR_BODY=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.description // ""')

gh pr create --base "main" --head "$BRANCH" --title "$PR_TITLE" --body "$(cat <<EOF
$PR_BODY

---

Closes $BEAD_ID (polecat-pr).
EOF
)"
```

Capture the PR URL for the close-note:
```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

If `gh pr create` fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: gh pr create failed" -m "Issue: ga-w1u. Branch: $BRANCH. PR did not open."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**5. Close the bead with a pointer to the PR:**
```bash
bd update ga-w1u --notes "PR opened: $PR_URL (branch: $BRANCH)"
bd close ga-w1u
```

**6. Tear down the worktree.**

The worktree lives on until here so restarts during push/PR can resume.
Now it's safe to remove:
```bash
WORKTREE_PATH=$(pwd)
cd ..
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE_PATH" --force || rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-w1u --unset-metadata work_dir
# Leave metadata.branch intact — it's useful post-merge for traceability.
```

**7. Signal reconciler and exit.**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
exit
```

`gc runtime drain-ack` tells the reconciler to kill this session. The
reconciler only restarts you if the pool check command finds more work.
You are GONE. Done means gone. There is no idle state.

**Exit criteria:** Feature branch pushed, PR opened against
main, worktree removed, bead closed, session exited.

title: Set up worktree on a feature branch
description:
Create an isolated worktree checked out on a new feature branch off
base_branch. The feature branch will be pushed and turned into a PR
in the terminal step.

**Config: base_branch = main**
**Config: setup_command = **

**1. Fetch latest:**
```bash
git fetch --prune origin
```

**2. Resolve worktree path and branch name from metadata (resume case).**

Check if `metadata.work_dir` already records your worktree path, and if
`metadata.branch` records your feature branch name:
```bash
WORKTREE=$(bd show ga-uxi --json | jq -r '.metadata.work_dir // empty')
BRANCH=$(bd show ga-uxi --json | jq -r '.metadata.branch // empty')
```

**If worktree path exists in metadata and directory still exists** — reuse it:
```bash
cd "$WORKTREE"
git fetch origin main
# If branch has unpushed local work, rebase it on base_branch; else stay on branch
git rebase origin/main
```
If the directory is missing, fall through to create a new one.

**If no worktree** — create one:
```bash
# Derive a branch name if not already recorded
if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
    SLUG=$(echo "ga-uxi" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -c 'a-z0-9-' '-' | sed 's/-\+/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//')
    BRANCH="users/${GC_AGENT:-polecat}/${SLUG}"
fi

WORKTREE_PATH=$(mktemp -d /tmp/polecat-ga-uxi-XXXXXX)
rmdir "$WORKTREE_PATH"
# Create feature branch off latest base_branch, check out in the new worktree
git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" "origin/main"
cd "$WORKTREE_PATH"
```

Record immediately so restarts and witness recovery can find both:
```bash
bd update ga-uxi --set-metadata work_dir="$WORKTREE_PATH"

```

title: mol-polecat-pr
description:
Polecat PR-review variant — commits to a feature branch and opens a pull
request against base_branch.

Extends mol-polecat-base with a feature-branch workspace setup (isolated
/tmp worktree + branch off base_branch) and a commit-push-PR terminal
step instead of direct-to-base commit. Designed for cities that use
human or automated PR review (e.g., MCDClient) before merge.

## Polecat Contract (PR-Review Model)

1. Receive work (molecule poured with this formula, assigned to you)
2. Follow steps in order (read descriptions, execute, move to next)
3. Commit to feature branch, push, open PR, close bead, exit
4. You are GONE — review + merge happens downstream

**Feature branch + PR.** Work lands on `users/<agent>/<issue-slug>`
branch in a /tmp worktree that is isolated from the shared rig tree.
The PR is opened against base_branch via `gh pr create`.

## Why /tmp worktrees

Pool agents sharing a single rig working tree stomp each other: one
agent's `git checkout <branch>` wipes another agent's untracked files
or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

title: Push feature branch and open PR
description:
Push your feature branch to origin and open a PR against base_branch.
You cease to exist after this step.

**1. Final clean-state verification (safeguard):**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
If ANY output (untracked files, uncommitted changes):
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "chore: capture remaining work (ga-1uk)"
```

**2. Rebase on latest base_branch (one last time) to minimize PR merge conflicts:**
```bash
git fetch origin main
git rebase origin/main
# If rebase conflicts: resolve them, then git rebase --continue. If
# conflicts are unresolvable, mail witness and drain-ack.
```

**3. Push the feature branch:**
```bash
BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
```

If push fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Push failed" -m "Issue: ga-1uk. Branch: $BRANCH. Cannot push to origin."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**4. Open the PR via gh:**

Derive a title and body from the bead. Keep the title short (≤70 chars);
put detail in the body.
```bash
BEAD_TITLE=$(bd show ga-1uk --json | jq -r '.title')
BEAD_ID="ga-1uk"

# Title: "<bead title> (<bead id>)" — truncate if too long
PR_TITLE="${BEAD_TITLE} (${BEAD_ID})"

# Body: ID + full description + footer
PR_BODY=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.description // ""')

gh pr create --base "main" --head "$BRANCH" --title "$PR_TITLE" --body "$(cat <<EOF
$PR_BODY

---

Closes $BEAD_ID (polecat-pr).
EOF
)"
```

Capture the PR URL for the close-note:
```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

If `gh pr create` fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: gh pr create failed" -m "Issue: ga-1uk. Branch: $BRANCH. PR did not open."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**5. Close the bead with a pointer to the PR:**
```bash
bd update ga-1uk --notes "PR opened: $PR_URL (branch: $BRANCH)"
bd close ga-1uk
```

**6. Tear down the worktree.**

The worktree lives on until here so restarts during push/PR can resume.
Now it's safe to remove:
```bash
WORKTREE_PATH=$(pwd)
cd ..
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE_PATH" --force || rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-1uk --unset-metadata work_dir
# Leave metadata.branch intact — it's useful post-merge for traceability.
```

**7. Signal reconciler and exit.**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
exit
```

`gc runtime drain-ack` tells the reconciler to kill this session. The
reconciler only restarts you if the pool check command finds more work.
You are GONE. Done means gone. There is no idle state.

**Exit criteria:** Feature branch pushed, PR opened against
main, worktree removed, bead closed, session exited.

title: Load context and verify assignment
description:
Initialize your session and understand your assignment.

**1. Prime your environment:**
```bash
gc prime                    # Load role context
bd prime                    # Load beads context
```

**2. Check your hook:**
```bash
bd list --assignee=$GC_AGENT --status=in_progress
```

The hook_bead is your assigned issue. Read it carefully:
```bash
bd show ga-bxq           # Full issue details
bd show ga-bxq --json | jq '.metadata'  # Check for existing metadata
```

**3. Check for rejection (IMPORTANT):**

If `metadata.rejection_reason` exists, this bead was previously attempted
and rejected by the refinery. Read the reason carefully:
- Rebase conflict → you'll resume the existing branch and rebase
- Test failure → you'll resume the branch and fix the issue

If `metadata.branch` exists, a branch already exists from the prior attempt.
You will use it in workspace-setup instead of creating a new one.

**4. Check inbox for additional context:**
```bash
gc mail inbox
# Read any HANDOFF or assignment messages, then archive after absorbing context
# gc mail read <id> → process → gc mail archive <id>
```

**5. Understand the requirements:**
- What exactly needs to be done?
- What files are likely involved?
- Are there dependencies or blockers?
- What does "done" look like?
- If rejected: what specifically needs fixing?

If blocked or unclear, mail Witness:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Unclear requirements" -m "Issue: ga-bxq
Question: <what you need clarified>"
```

**Exit criteria:** You understand the work and can begin.

title: mol-polecat-pr
description:
Polecat PR-review variant — commits to a feature branch and opens a pull
request against base_branch.

Extends mol-polecat-base with a feature-branch workspace setup (isolated
/tmp worktree + branch off base_branch) and a commit-push-PR terminal
step instead of direct-to-base commit. Designed for cities that use
human or automated PR review (e.g., MCDClient) before merge.

## Polecat Contract (PR-Review Model)

1. Receive work (molecule poured with this formula, assigned to you)
2. Follow steps in order (read descriptions, execute, move to next)
3. Commit to feature branch, push, open PR, close bead, exit
4. You are GONE — review + merge happens downstream

**Feature branch + PR.** Work lands on `users/<agent>/<issue-slug>`
branch in a /tmp worktree that is isolated from the shared rig tree.
The PR is opened against base_branch via `gh pr create`.

## Why /tmp worktrees

Pool agents sharing a single rig working tree stomp each other: one
agent's `git checkout <branch>` wipes another agent's untracked files
or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

title: Push feature branch and open PR
description:
Push your feature branch to origin and open a PR against base_branch.
You cease to exist after this step.

**1. Final clean-state verification (safeguard):**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
If ANY output (untracked files, uncommitted changes):
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "chore: capture remaining work (ga-imo)"
```

**2. Rebase on latest base_branch (one last time) to minimize PR merge conflicts:**
```bash
git fetch origin main
git rebase origin/main
# If rebase conflicts: resolve them, then git rebase --continue. If
# conflicts are unresolvable, mail witness and drain-ack.
```

**3. Push the feature branch:**
```bash
BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
```

If push fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Push failed" -m "Issue: ga-imo. Branch: $BRANCH. Cannot push to origin."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**4. Open the PR via gh:**

Derive a title and body from the bead. Keep the title short (≤70 chars);
put detail in the body.
```bash
BEAD_TITLE=$(bd show ga-imo --json | jq -r '.title')
BEAD_ID="ga-imo"

# Title: "<bead title> (<bead id>)" — truncate if too long
PR_TITLE="${BEAD_TITLE} (${BEAD_ID})"

# Body: ID + full description + footer
PR_BODY=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.description // ""')

gh pr create --base "main" --head "$BRANCH" --title "$PR_TITLE" --body "$(cat <<EOF
$PR_BODY

---

Closes $BEAD_ID (polecat-pr).
EOF
)"
```

Capture the PR URL for the close-note:
```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

If `gh pr create` fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: gh pr create failed" -m "Issue: ga-imo. Branch: $BRANCH. PR did not open."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**5. Close the bead with a pointer to the PR:**
```bash
bd update ga-imo --notes "PR opened: $PR_URL (branch: $BRANCH)"
bd close ga-imo
```

**6. Tear down the worktree.**

The worktree lives on until here so restarts during push/PR can resume.
Now it's safe to remove:
```bash
WORKTREE_PATH=$(pwd)
cd ..
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE_PATH" --force || rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-imo --unset-metadata work_dir
# Leave metadata.branch intact — it's useful post-merge for traceability.
```

**7. Signal reconciler and exit.**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
exit
```

`gc runtime drain-ack` tells the reconciler to kill this session. The
reconciler only restarts you if the pool check command finds more work.
You are GONE. Done means gone. There is no idle state.

**Exit criteria:** Feature branch pushed, PR opened against
main, worktree removed, bead closed, session exited.

title: Set up worktree on a feature branch
description:
Create an isolated worktree checked out on a new feature branch off
base_branch. The feature branch will be pushed and turned into a PR
in the terminal step.

**Config: base_branch = main**
**Config: setup_command = **

**1. Fetch latest:**
```bash
git fetch --prune origin
```

**2. Resolve worktree path and branch name from metadata (resume case).**

Check if `metadata.work_dir` already records your worktree path, and if
`metadata.branch` records your feature branch name:
```bash
WORKTREE=$(bd show ga-imo --json | jq -r '.metadata.work_dir // empty')
BRANCH=$(bd show ga-imo --json | jq -r '.metadata.branch // empty')
```

**If worktree path exists in metadata and directory still exists** — reuse it:
```bash
cd "$WORKTREE"
git fetch origin main
# If branch has unpushed local work, rebase it on base_branch; else stay on branch
git rebase origin/main
```
If the directory is missing, fall through to create a new one.

**If no worktree** — create one:
```bash
# Derive a branch name if not already recorded
if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
    SLUG=$(echo "ga-imo" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -c 'a-z0-9-' '-' | sed 's/-\+/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//')
    BRANCH="users/${GC_AGENT:-polecat}/${SLUG}"
fi

WORKTREE_PATH=$(mktemp -d /tmp/polecat-ga-imo-XXXXXX)
rmdir "$WORKTREE_PATH"
# Create feature branch off latest base_branch, check out in the new worktree
git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" "origin/main"
cd "$WORKTREE_PATH"
```

Record immediately so restarts and witness recovery can find both:
```bash
bd update ga-imo --set-metadata work_dir="$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-imo --set-metadata branch="$BRANCH"
```

**3. Ensure clean working state:**
```bash
git status                  # Should be clean
git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD   # Should show your feature branch
```

**4. Run project setup (if configured):**
```bash

```
Empty setup_command → skip.

**Exit criteria:** In your `/tmp/polecat-*` worktree, on your feature
branch, deps installed, work_dir + branch recorded on the bead.

or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

title: gc BEADS_ACTOR inconsistent across session spawn paths
description:
**Symptom**: `BEADS_ACTOR` (what `bd update --claim` uses for the assignee) has different formats depending on how the session was spawned:

| Session origin | GC_SESSION_NAME / BEADS_ACTOR | Example |
|----------------|--------------------------------|---------|
| ephemeral (pool) | `<template-basename>-<session-id>` | `designer-lgf-b807` |
| manual (user-spawned named) | `s-<session-id>` | `s-lgf-jtvr` |
| named always-on (singleton) | `<rig>/<template>` sometimes | `claude-multiplex/witness` |

**Why it matters**:

1. Cross-session recovery breaks — an agent's tier-1 query `bd list --assignee=$GC_TEMPLATE` won't find beads the session previously claimed if `--claim` stored the assignee as `s-lgf-jtvr`.
2. Bead history becomes cryptic — `s-lgf-jtvr` tells a future reader nothing about the agent's role.
3. Pack authors have to guess which identity to use in prompts ($GC_TEMPLATE vs $BEADS_ACTOR vs $GC_AGENT). Today the prompt templates assume $GC_TEMPLATE is consistent — it is — but claims go via $BEADS_ACTOR which ISN'T.

**Desired outcome**: single convention for BEADS_ACTOR across all spawn paths. For any scope=rig singleton agent (max_active_sessions=1), ideal is BEADS_ACTOR == $GC_TEMPLATE (e.g. `claude-multiplex/builder`). For pool agents, could be `<template>-<N>` or `<template>-<session-id>` — but predictable and documented.

**Repro**:

    # Pool spawn path (ephemeral)
    pgrep -af '\[l1-gc-factory\] claude-multiplex/designer' | head -1 \
        | xargs -I{} cat /proc/{}/environ | tr '\0' '\n' | grep BEADS_ACTOR
    # Manual spawn path
    pgrep -af '\[l1-gc-factory\] claude-multiplex/builder' | head -1 \
        | xargs -I{} cat /proc/{}/environ | tr '\0' '\n' | grep BEADS_ACTOR

Both are reading from a running session, both are scope=rig agents in the same rig, but the BEADS_ACTOR format differs.

**Related fix already landed**: pack prompts were updated to `bd update <id> --claim --assignee="$GC_TEMPLATE"` so claims pin assignee to the role. That assumes `--claim` respects the `--assignee` override — if it DOESN'T, that fix is a no-op and this bug is the only path forward.

**Low priority** — deferred until network/env is less noisy. Not blocking anything urgent.

**Note**: also worth flagging (but don't investigate now per human's call) — a sling to `l1-gc-factory/claude-multiplex/builder` didn't auto-spawn the session; user had to wake it manually. That may be related (pool budget, route caching) or unrelated. Record it here, investigate later.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Single BEADS_ACTOR convention documented across spawn paths. 2) For singleton named sessions (max_active=1), BEADS_ACTOR == GC_TEMPLATE. 3) --claim --assignee behavior documented. 4) Root cause located in gc code (spawn path that sets SESSION_NAME / BEADS_ACTOR).
notes: Investigation complete. Root cause: cmd/gc/template_resolve.go:223 sets BEADS_ACTOR=sessName once. The named-session overrides in build_desired_state.go:386-389 and session_reconciler.go:1095-1098 rewrite GC_AGENT/GC_ALIAS/GC_TEMPLATE to the raw identity but leave BEADS_ACTOR pointing at the sanitized sessName, so in a named session BEADS_ACTOR != GC_AGENT. Verified empirically in this investigator session (BEADS_ACTOR=gascity--investigator, GC_AGENT=gascity/investigator). Also confirmed empirically that 'bd update --claim --assignee=X' honors X (overrides BEADS_ACTOR default). Fix handed to builder via ga-6fo.

title: Replace per-tick fingerprint recomputation with config-reload diff
description:
## Context

The reconciler's config-drift detection compares a stored fingerprint
(computed at session start) against a per-tick recomputation via
`runtime.CoreFingerprint(templateParamsToConfig(tp))`. The recomputation
depends on transient I/O — specifically `LoadCityCatalog` reading the
filesystem every tick in `newAgentBuildParams`. This produces
non-deterministic fingerprints when:

- `LoadCityCatalog` returns an error (filesystem race, permissions flap).
- `LoadCityCatalog` returns an empty catalog despite the cfg referencing
  skills (observed in gc-management — cfg state briefly flaps during a
  config-reload window).

PR #910 patches this with a process-level last-good cache: a load failure
or empty result reuses the prior cached catalog so the fingerprint stays
stable. This is load-bearing for session stability (without it, every
live session drains in a config-drift storm every few ticks) but it's a
workaround for a deeper design flaw.

## Proposal

Fingerprints should be deterministic functions of *declared* config,
never of transient I/O. The `CityRuntime.reloadConfig` path already
tracks a `configRev` revision that's stable within a reload cycle; drift
detection should hang off that instead of per-tick hash recomputation.

Sketch:

1. On session start, record the full `TemplateParams` (or a hash of just
   the identity-relevant fields) in session metadata — same as today.
2. On config reload success, diff old vs new `config.City` for
   identity-relevant fields (Command, included Env keys, WakeMode, pool
   bounds, DependsOn, skill entries, CopyFiles ContentHash). Mark
   affected session templates as "needs restart".
3. Reconciler reads the "needs restart" flag, drains affected sessions,
   clears the flag.
4. No per-tick hash recomputation. No filesystem I/O in the drift path.

## Benefits

- Fingerprinting is no longer I/O-dependent → no more "transient failure
  drains everything" storms.
- One diff computation per reload instead of one fingerprint per session
  per tick (performance).
- Clearer semantics: drift fires when config *actually changed*, not when
  the filesystem briefly hiccupped.
- Eliminates the whole skill-catalog-cache workaround.

## Related

- PR #910 — cache workaround that ships for 1.0
- PR #833 — dropped pool.check from FingerprintExtra (same class of bug,
  narrower fix)
- PR #869 — ignored GC_ALIAS (same class, narrower)
- Open issue #460 — "Pool scale-up from min=0 never spawns polecats for
  labeled work + config-drift drain storm (ga-7xq)"

## Scope / timing

Target post-1.0 — this is a structural cleanup, not a stability fix.
#910 already resolves the user-visible breakage.

value: bd create --deps 'blocked-by:X' stores a dep of TYPE blocked-by which bd ready IGNORES as a blocker. Only deps of TYPE blocks (from 'bd dep add CHILD PARENT' or --deps 'blocks:X' where the new bead is the blocker) are respected by bd ready. When child-blocked-by-parent relationships are needed for readiness gating, always use 'bd dep add <dependent> <prerequisite>' after create, or use --deps 'blocks:<new-dependent-id>' at create time on the prerequisite.
